summary: Ukrainian UAVs conducted multiple strikes across Odesa, Kharkiv, Sumy and the Black Sea coast, causing civilian injuries and infrastructure damage. Russian forces reported shooting down 62 Ukrainian UAVs overnight and observed increased military transport activity, including AN‑124 deliveries to Russian airfields. Negotiations on prisoner exchanges and air‑defence aid continue amid ongoing frontline combat.
key_points: Ukrainian strike drones hit Odesa, injuring four civilians and damaging residential buildings, a post office and a food store (Source: @UkraineNow/81489).
Multiple Russian‑controlled UAVs were observed over Odesa region, Zatoka and Chornomorsk from the sea (Source: @eRadarrua/93995).
Russian Ministry of Defence reported downing 62 Ukrainian UAVs over Russian regions during one night (Source: @tass_agency/377851).
AN‑124-100 landed at Dyagilevo airfield, likely delivering a batch of H‑22/32 missiles; AN‑72 and IL‑76MD also active at Engels, Shatalovo, Baltiysk and Kursk (Source: @eRadarrua/93991).
Ukrainian forces liberated the village of Vozdvizhevka in Zaporizhzhia region, a key point on the defensive line west of the Gaichur River (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30469).
Ukrainian intelligence indicated ongoing negotiations for a "1000 on 1000" prisoner exchange, including long‑detained and seriously injured captives (Source: @UkraineNow/81490).
Russian reports of UAV attacks on Sevastopol noted 14 drones shot down and subsequent missile launch at a power station, with air defence preventing outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian UAV activity intensified, striking Odesa and coastal targets while Russian air defenses claimed high UAV losses. Russian transport aircraft delivered missile payloads to multiple airfields, indicating preparation for further strikes. Frontline battles continue with Ukrainian gains in Zaporizhzhia and ongoing prisoner‑exchange talks.

points: Ukrainian strike drones hit Odesa, injuring four civilians and damaging residential buildings, a post office and a food store, with several private cars catching fire (Source: @UkraineNow/81489).
Ukrainian forces liberated Vozdvizhevka village in Zaporizhzhia region, described as a key point on the enemy defensive line west of the Gaichur River (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30469).

Ukrainian intelligence reported that negotiations for a "1000 on 1000" prisoner exchange are ongoing, potentially including detainees held since 2022 and seriously injured captives (Source: @UkraineNow/81490).
Multiple UAV groups were observed over Kharkiv, Sumy and Poltava regions, including strike drones heading toward Bilozirka, Zinkiv and Opishnia (Source: @eRadarrua/93992; @kpszsu/63033).
Russian Ministry of Defence announced that 62 Ukrainian UAVs were shot down over Russian territory during a single night (Source: @tass_agency/377851).

AN‑124‑100 landed at Dyagilevo airfield, likely delivering H‑22/32 missiles; AN‑72 and IL‑76MD aircraft were also active at Engels, Shatalovo, Baltiysk and Kursk, suggesting transport of ballistic or cruise missiles and strike UAVs (Source: @eRadarrua/93991).
Russian officials reported damage to the regional court building in Nizhny Novgorod from a UAV attack, with no immediate casualties (Source: @tass_agency/377861).
Russian reports indicated that Ukrainian UAVs over the Black Sea were marked with fascist symbols (Source: @tass_agency/377847).
Ukrainian strike drones were reported in the Donetsk region, targeting a passenger bus route between Makeyevka and Sevastopol (Source: @tass_agency/377823).
Ukrainian UAV activity was noted in the Sumy front, with groups operating near Królevets and Shostka, and firefights reported around Lesnoye and Taratutino (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Russian forces claimed advances of up to 900 meters in twenty‑two sectors on the Sumy front, pushing Ukrainian units out of Zapselye (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed heading toward Bilhorod‑Dnistrovskyi from the south (Source: @kpszsu/63032).
Ukrainian UAV groups were reported in the northern Sumy area, moving southwest (Source: @kpszsu/63019).
Russian reports indicated increased aircraft, artillery and strike UAV usage in the Konstantinovka area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
No specific Ukrainian ground actions were detailed for the northern front in the provided messages.
No additional verifiable data for the northern front.
Ukrainian strike drones attacked Odesa, causing civilian injuries and property damage (Source: @UkraineNow/81489).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed over the Black Sea coast, heading toward Zatoka and Chornomorsk from the sea (Source: @eRadarrua/93995).
Russian reports of a UAV attack on Sevastopol noted 14 drones shot down and a missile launch at a power station, with air defence preventing outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
No further specific southern front ground actions were detailed in the messages.
Russian air defence shot down 62 Ukrainian UAVs overnight across Russian regions (Source: @tass_agency/377851).
Ukrainian UAV attacks on Sevastopol resulted in 14 drones being downed and a subsequent missile strike at a power station, with no outages reported (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
AN‑124‑100, AN‑72 and IL‑76MD aircraft were active delivering possible missile payloads to Russian airfields, indicating logistical buildup (Source: @eRadarrua/93991).
Ukrainian UAV activity was reported over multiple oblasts, including Kharkiv, Sumy, Poltava and Odesa, targeting both military and civilian infrastructure (Source: @eRadarrua/93992; @kpszsu/63033).

key_developments: Ukrainian strike drones hit Odesa, injuring four civilians and damaging residential buildings, a post office and a food store (Source: @UkraineNow/81489).
Russian Ministry of Defence reported downing 62 Ukrainian UAVs during a single night across Russian territory (Source: @tass_agency/377851).
AN‑124‑100 landed at Dyagilevo airfield, likely delivering H‑22/32 missiles; AN‑72 and IL‑76MD also operated at several Russian airfields (Source: @eRadarrua/93991).
Ukrainian forces liberated the village of Vozdvizhevka in Zaporizhzhia region, a key point on the defensive line west of the Gaichur River (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30469).
Ukrainian intelligence indicated ongoing negotiations for a "1000 on 1000" prisoner exchange, including long‑detained and seriously injured captives (Source: @UkraineNow/81490).
Ukrainian UAVs over the Black Sea were reported heading toward Zatoka and Chornomorsk from the sea (Source: @eRadarrua/93995).
Ukrainian UAV attacks on Sevastopol resulted in 14 drones shot down and a missile strike at a power station, with air defence preventing outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV operations are expanding across multiple fronts, targeting both civilian infrastructure in Odesa and strategic coastal points, indicating a shift toward broader interdiction. Russian air defence claims high UAV attrition, yet continued deliveries of missile payloads via heavy transport aircraft suggest preparation for intensified strike campaigns. The ongoing prisoner‑exchange negotiations reflect a diplomatic dimension that may influence operational tempo.
sections.regional_response: Western partners are being urged for additional air‑defence systems, as highlighted by Ukrainian officials, while Russian authorities emphasize UAV losses and portray defensive successes. Local authorities in affected Ukrainian cities report civilian casualties and infrastructure damage, prompting emergency responses. Russian regional administrations note infrastructure impacts from UAV attacks but report limited casualties.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths reveals coordinated attacks from sea‑based launch points toward Odesa and inland oblasts, requiring enhanced radar coverage. The arrival of AN‑124‑type cargo suggests a forthcoming increase in long‑range missile deployments. Prisoner‑exchange talks may serve as leverage for future ceasefire or de‑escalation initiatives. Russian claims of high UAV kill rates should be cross‑checked with independent satellite and SIGINT sources for verification.
